C[mnemo: so briefly put the point is that you can expect there to be difference in ranks in different places, and it's hatrd to tell what might be causing teh difference, since there's plenty of possibilities
C[mnemo: for ane xample let's say I have a group of 4 in a local chess club  or something like that
C[mnemo: we play among each other and the elo format gives us ratings of 1200,1300,1400,1500  or something like that
C[mnemo: then the guy who has 1500 rating, goes to a neighbouring city, and loses 3 games and goes down to 1420
C[mnemo: then he comes back and plays with the other guys, and then each one of those people will also have their rank adjusted
C[mnemo: but that's when the ranks start to correlate which each other
C[Plunder: I can imagine ELO, or any other rating system for that matter, doesn't really work very well between isolated groups
